sources.title: Recommendations for the Conduct, Reporting, Editing, and Publication of Scholarly Work in Medical Journals: Preparing a Manuscript for Submission to a Medical Journal
sources.journal_or_body: International Committee of Medical Journal Editors
sources.license: link to official current version; do not republish
sources.evidence_scope: biomedical manuscript structure, abstract consistency, limitations, figures/tables metadata, registration and data identifiers
sources.notes: Official current version is hosted by ICMJE; ASCReview should cite/link rather than store full recommendation text.

sources.title: EQUATOR Network Library for Health Research Reporting
sources.evidence_scope: selecting reporting guidelines by study design
sources.notes: Use as a routing index; verify each linked guideline and license separately.

sources.title: How to write a great research paper using reporting guidelines
sources.evidence_scope: review questions for method transparency, reproducibility, extractable numerical results, and intervention detail
sources.notes: Useful for review checklists; do not copy long page text into corpus.

sources.title: PMC Open Access Subset
sources.journal_or_body: National Library of Medicine / PubMed Central
sources.evidence_scope: corpus storage and reuse classification
sources.notes: PMC OA subset includes commercial-use, non-commercial-only, and other terms groups. Verify article-level license.

sources.title: PMC Copyright Notice
sources.journal_or_body: National Library of Medicine / PubMed Central
sources.evidence_scope: distinguishing free-to-read PMC records from reusable open access records
sources.notes: Free access does not automatically mean broad reuse; check CC-like license and article record.

sources.title: The PRISMA 2020 statement: an updated guideline for reporting systematic reviews
sources.license: CC BY 4.0 for statement and explanation documents per PRISMA citing page
sources.evidence_scope: systematic review reporting, abstract/checklist/flow diagram structure
sources.notes: Use only when the task is a systematic review or review-methods section; not a generic dissertation rubric.

sources.title: The Strengthening the Reporting of Observational Studies in Epidemiology (STROBE) Statement
sources.evidence_scope: observational study title, introduction, methods, results, discussion, and funding reporting
sources.notes: Use for cohort, case-control, and cross-sectional study reporting; pair with explanatory article where needed.

sources.title: CONSORT 2010 Statement: Updated Guidelines for Reporting Parallel Group Randomised Trials
sources.evidence_scope: randomized trial reporting, checklist, participant flow, explicit descriptions of what was done and found
sources.notes: Apply only to randomized trial designs or relevant trial discussion.

sources.title: Reporting recommendations for tumor marker prognostic studies (REMARK): explanation and elaboration
sources.evidence_scope: tumor marker/prognostic biomarker reporting, objectives, specimens, assays, statistical analysis, limitations
sources.notes: Highly relevant to cancer biomarker writing; do not apply to all cancer dissertations by default.

sources.title: The MDAR (Materials Design Analysis Reporting) Framework for transparent reporting in the life sciences
sources.evidence_scope: materials, design, analysis, and reporting transparency in life sciences
sources.notes: Use as metadata/pattern support unless license is rechecked in a future expansion.

sources.title: MIAME and MINSEQE guidelines
sources.license: official NCBI guidance page; metadata only
sources.evidence_scope: minimum information for microarray and high-throughput sequencing submissions
sources.notes: Useful for genomics methods and data availability review.

sources.title: Bioinformatics Author Guidelines
sources.evidence_scope: software, code, data, documentation, test data, stable URLs, data availability statement expectations
sources.notes: Use as a high bar for bioinformatics manuscripts, not as a dissertation rubric unless assigned.

sources.title: Ten simple rules for structuring papers
sources.evidence_scope: context-content-conclusion structure across abstract, introduction, results, and discussion
sources.notes: Use for writing structure patterns; not a biomedical evidence source.

sources.title: Ten Simple Rules for Better Figures
sources.evidence_scope: figure clarity, visual design, color, reproducible figure scripts
sources.notes: Use for figure review and legend expectations alongside task rubric.

sources.title: Ten Simple Rules for Reproducible Computational Research
sources.evidence_scope: linking claims to results, scripts/runs/results access, computational provenance
sources.notes: Use for bioinformatics methods and reproducibility checks.

sources.title: Ten Simple Rules for Effective Computational Research
sources.evidence_scope: computational research planning, code/data habits, reproducibility-oriented workflows
sources.notes: Use for methods-review heuristics; source is not a disease-domain authority.

sources.title: Ten Simple Rules for Effective Statistical Practice
sources.evidence_scope: statistical question framing, design-analysis alignment, caution in interpretation
sources.notes: Use as general statistical practice guidance; consult statistician for task-specific analysis validity.

sources.title: The ARRIVE guidelines 2.0: Updated guidelines for reporting animal research
sources.evidence_scope: in vivo animal research reporting, essential design details, sample size, inclusion/exclusion, randomization, blinding, outcomes, statistics, animal details
sources.notes: Apply only to in vivo animal studies or preclinical animal-model sections selected during specialization.

sources.title: Reporting animal research: Explanation and elaboration for the ARRIVE guidelines 2.0
sources.evidence_scope: animal-study interpretation, model limitations, bias, exploratory outcome caution, discussion of implications
sources.notes: Use for preclinical discussion and limitations checks; do not apply to non-animal studies.

sources.title: The FAIR Guiding Principles for scientific data management and stewardship
sources.evidence_scope: findable, accessible, interoperable, reusable data and metadata principles for datasets, tools, and workflows
sources.notes: High-level data stewardship principle; translate into concrete data/code review checks only through task context.

sources.title: Transparent Reporting of a multivariable prediction model for Individual Prognosis Or Diagnosis (TRIPOD): the TRIPOD Statement
sources.journal_or_body: TRIPOD / BMC Medicine / Annals of Internal Medicine
sources.evidence_scope: clinical diagnostic/prognostic prediction model reporting, model development, validation, performance, applicability
sources.notes: Use only when specialization identifies prediction-model development or validation.

sources.title: TRIPOD+AI statement: updated guidance for reporting clinical prediction models that use regression or machine learning methods
sources.evidence_scope: clinical prediction model reporting across regression and machine learning, including AI model transparency
sources.notes: Use only for clinical prediction model studies; not a generic bioinformatics checklist.

sources.title: Basic Statistical Reporting for Articles Published in Biomedical Journals: the SAMPL Guidelines
sources.journal_or_body: EQUATOR Network / European Association of Science Editors
sources.license: guideline page and PDF terms vary; metadata only
sources.evidence_scope: basic statistical reporting in biomedical journal articles
sources.notes: Use for statistical reporting completeness; do not treat as a substitute for a statistician or teacher rubric.

sources.title: Biospecimen Reporting for Improved Study Quality (BRISQ)
sources.license: author manuscript in PMC; verify reuse before excerpting
sources.evidence_scope: human biospecimen preanalytical variables, collection, stabilization, preservation, clinical/pathology context, missing biospecimen metadata implications
sources.notes: Highly relevant to cancer biomarker and human tissue studies; apply only when human biospecimens are in scope.

sources.title: Nature Portfolio reporting standards and availability of data, materials, code and protocols
sources.evidence_scope: data availability statements, accession numbers, controlled access explanations, data citation, code availability, materials and RRID reporting
sources.notes: Use as a journal-standard comparator, not as an automatic dissertation scoring rule.

sources.title: BMC Cancer submission guidelines: preparing your manuscript
sources.evidence_scope: data availability and materials statements, dataset citation, software/code metadata fields for cancer manuscripts
sources.notes: Useful as cancer-journal reporting comparator; not a universal dissertation rubric.

sources.title: STAR methods - Key Resources Table
sources.evidence_scope: key resources table, resource identifiers, RRIDs, software/data URLs, reproducibility metadata
sources.notes: Use for resource-traceability patterns in methods/figures; do not copy page text into corpus.

sources.title: JCI Author Information Center
sources.evidence_scope: data availability, supporting data values, code commit IDs, large dataset repository deposition, material sharing, sex-as-biological-variable examples
sources.notes: Use as a biomedical journal-standard comparator with careful rubric priority caveat.

sources.title: Moderated estimation of fold change and dispersion for RNA-seq data with DESeq2
sources.evidence_scope: RNA-seq count-data method reporting, count matrices, normalization, dispersion/fold-change estimation, design-aware analysis
sources.notes: Use as a source pattern for RNA-seq methods reporting, not as a mandate to use DESeq2.

sources.title: Integrative Analysis of Complex Cancer Genomics and Clinical Profiles Using the cBioPortal
sources.license: author manuscript in PMC; verify reuse before excerpting
sources.evidence_scope: public cancer genomics portal analysis, multidimensional cancer genomics data, clinical profile integration, survival and alteration-query limitations
sources.notes: Use as metadata and source-pattern support for public cancer-genomics analyses; do not treat exploratory portal analysis as validated mechanism.

sources.title: The Molecular Taxonomy of Primary Prostate Cancer
sources.journal_or_body: Cell / The Cancer Genome Atlas Research Network
sources.license: Cell article/free PMC record; metadata only unless reuse is separately verified
sources.evidence_scope: optional prostate cancer genomics seed for future specialization, molecular taxonomy and DNA repair alteration context
sources.notes: Optional domain seed only; must not be hardcoded into ASCReview Core.

sources.title: Integrative clinical genomics of advanced prostate cancer
sources.license: Cell article/free PMC record; metadata only unless reuse is separately verified
sources.evidence_scope: optional metastatic/advanced prostate cancer genomics seed for future specialization, including integrative sequencing and resistance-context reasoning
sources.notes: Optional domain seed only; not a generic ASCReview rule source.

sources.title: The REporting of studies Conducted using Observational Routinely-collected health Data (RECORD) Statement
sources.evidence_scope: routinely collected health data, database linkage, codes/algorithms, data access and cleaning transparency, STROBE extension logic
sources.notes: Use only for routinely collected observational health data or analogous registry/database analyses; do not apply to all observational or public-data work.

sources.title: Sex and Gender Equity in Research: rationale for the SAGER guidelines and recommended use
sources.journal_or_body: Research Integrity and Peer Review / EASE
sources.evidence_scope: sex and gender reporting in design, analysis, results, and interpretation when relevant to humans, animals, cells, tissues, or translational claims
sources.notes: Apply relevance-gated checks; absence of sex/gender analysis is not automatically a fatal flaw unless the study context makes it material.

sources.title: NIH Data Management and Sharing Policy Overview
sources.evidence_scope: scientific data management, sharing plans, validation/replication rationale, legal/proprietary/privacy constraints
sources.notes: Use to distinguish data-sharing expectations from misconduct claims; do not imply the policy applies to non-NIH-funded dissertation work unless specified.

sources.title: PLOS Open Science Policies: data availability and materials, software, and code sharing
sources.evidence_scope: data availability statements, replication data, legal/ethical restrictions, materials/software/code availability
sources.notes: Use as journal-policy support, not as a universal dissertation requirement.

sources.title: Gene Expression Omnibus
sources.evidence_scope: functional genomics accession IDs, Series/Platform/Sample traceability, MIAME-compliant submissions, public expression data reuse caveats
sources.notes: Use for public expression-data provenance checks; repository presence does not guarantee uniform processing or analysis validity.

sources.title: NCI Genomic Data Commons: Access Data
sources.evidence_scope: cancer genomic and clinical data access, portal/API/tool provenance, controlled-access caveats, case/file/query traceability
sources.notes: Use for public cancer-genomics data provenance; do not treat portal access as evidence of biological causality.

sources.title: ENCODE Data Standards
sources.evidence_scope: assay-specific data quality metrics, replicate concordance, read depth, antibody characterization, pipeline-specific QC standards
sources.notes: Use as a pattern for assay-specific QC logic; do not generalize one assay's thresholds to unrelated data types.

sources.title: AACR Project GENIE
sources.evidence_scope: real-world clinico-genomic cancer registry, harmonized multi-institutional data, registry-source limitations, precision-oncology exploratory analyses
sources.notes: Optional cancer-genomics source seed; it supports public registry provenance checks but not generic clinical-efficacy claims.

sources.title: A survey of best practices for RNA-seq data analysis
sources.evidence_scope: RNA-seq experimental design, quality control, alignment/quantification, visualization, differential expression, alternative splicing, fusion and eQTL analysis reporting
sources.notes: Use as a broad RNA-seq methods-pattern source; do not convert workflow options into mandatory software choices.

sources.title: limma powers differential expression analyses for RNA-sequencing and microarray studies
sources.evidence_scope: model-aware differential expression, design matrices, contrasts, covariates, weights, mean-variance modelling, microarray/RNA-seq crossover methods
sources.notes: Use as a source pattern for reporting model design and contrasts, not as a mandate to use limma.

sources.title: The MIQE guidelines: minimum information for publication of quantitative real-time PCR experiments
sources.evidence_scope: qPCR/RT-qPCR reporting, sample handling, RNA quality, primer/probe information, normalization, assay conditions
sources.notes: Use as metadata-only guideline support for qPCR methods checks; do not store article text unless license is separately verified.

sources.title: A comprehensive assessment of RNA-seq accuracy, reproducibility and information content by the Sequencing Quality Control Consortium
sources.evidence_scope: RNA-seq benchmark design, cross-platform reproducibility, reference samples, qPCR validation, differential-expression reproducibility cautions
sources.notes: Use as benchmark metadata only; do not extract full-text patterns unless reuse is verified through an open version.

sources.title: STARD 2015: an updated list of essential items for reporting diagnostic accuracy studies
sources.license: BMJ guideline article; metadata only unless reuse terms are separately verified
sources.evidence_scope: diagnostic accuracy study reporting, index test/reference standard, participant flow, estimates of diagnostic accuracy, uncertainty, and applicability
sources.notes: Use only for diagnostic accuracy work; do not apply to all biomarker or prediction studies.

sources.title: Better reporting of interventions: template for intervention description and replication (TIDieR) checklist and guide
sources.license: BMJ guideline article; metadata only unless reuse terms are separately verified
sources.evidence_scope: intervention description, materials, procedures, provider, mode, location, dose/intensity, tailoring, modifications, fidelity, comparator details
sources.notes: Use for intervention or comparator description checks; do not use as a generic Methods requirement.

sources.title: Current best practices in single-cell RNA-seq analysis: a tutorial
sources.evidence_scope: single-cell RNA-seq workflow reporting, count matrix provenance, QC covariates, filtering decisions, normalization, batch correction, feature selection, dimensionality reduction, clustering, marker testing, trajectory and gene-level analyses
sources.notes: Use as a source-pattern checklist for scRNA-seq reporting; do not convert the tutorial into a mandatory pipeline.

sources.title: SCANPY: large-scale single-cell gene expression data analysis
sources.license: open access article; use metadata and source patterns, verify article-level license before storing excerpts
sources.evidence_scope: single-cell analysis object/provenance patterns, preprocessing, visualization, clustering, pseudotime, differential-expression testing, software/code availability, benchmark/data traceability
sources.notes: Use as single-cell software/reporting pattern, not as a mandate to use Scanpy.

sources.title: Statistical and machine learning methods for spatially resolved transcriptomics data analysis
sources.evidence_scope: spatial transcriptomics reporting, spatial coordinates, tissue/image context, spot/cell resolution, segmentation/deconvolution, spatial domain detection, integration with scRNA-seq, spatial statistics and machine-learning limitations
sources.notes: Use as a spatial transcriptomics source pattern; do not generalize spatial-specific checks to bulk or ordinary single-cell work.

sources.title: The PRIDE database resources in 2022: a hub for mass spectrometry-based proteomics evidences
sources.evidence_scope: mass-spectrometry proteomics repository provenance, ProteomeXchange/PRIDE accession IDs, sample metadata, raw/processed files, Universal Spectrum Identifiers, dataset license, reanalysis caveats
sources.notes: Use for proteomics data provenance; repository deposition does not guarantee analysis reproducibility without methods and metadata.

sources.title: Clinical Proteomic Tumor Analysis Consortium (CPTAC)
sources.journal_or_body: National Cancer Institute / Genomic Data Commons / Proteomic Data Commons
sources.evidence_scope: cancer proteogenomics data access, GDC/PDC provenance, harmonized genomic and proteomic pipelines, controlled-access caveats, study accession and portal traceability
sources.notes: Use as cancer proteogenomics data-resource provenance; do not treat harmonized portal outputs as causal or clinical evidence by themselves.

sources.title: Ten Years of Pathway Analysis: Current Approaches and Outstanding Challenges
sources.evidence_scope: pathway/enrichment analysis generations, knowledge-base dependence, annotation limitations, topology and statistical assumptions, mechanistic interpretation cautions
sources.notes: Use to calibrate pathway/enrichment interpretation; enrichment does not prove mechanism.

sources.title: The Reactome pathway knowledgebase 2022
sources.license: Nucleic Acids Research open access article; Reactome data/source code use Creative Commons licenses per Reactome download page
sources.evidence_scope: curated human pathway knowledgebase, pathway IDs/releases, gene set analysis service, pathway annotation provenance, knowledgebase versioning and interpretation limits
sources.notes: Use as pathway resource provenance; verify database release/version and license before storing pathway content.

sources.title: Pan-cancer analysis of whole genomes
sources.journal_or_body: Nature / ICGC-TCGA Pan-Cancer Analysis of Whole Genomes Consortium
sources.evidence_scope: pan-cancer whole-genome analysis, cohort scale, driver discovery incompleteness, open/controlled data tiers, cloud and pipeline provenance, somatic variant and pathway interpretation limits
sources.notes: Use as generic cancer-genomics source pattern; not tied to the user's dissertation topic.

sources.title: The Cancer Genome Atlas Pan-Cancer analysis project
sources.journal_or_body: Nature Genetics / The Cancer Genome Atlas Research Network
sources.evidence_scope: multi-platform pan-cancer integration, cross-tumor comparison, tumor lineage commonalities/differences, molecular aberration interpretation, cross-cancer generalization limits
sources.notes: Use as pan-cancer integration pattern with non-commercial/share-alike caution; not a dissertation-specific source.

sources.title: Pathway and network analysis of more than 2500 whole cancer genomes
sources.license: Nature Communications open access article; verify article-level license before excerpt storage
sources.evidence_scope: cancer pathway and network interpretation, driver/pathway aggregation, whole-genome cohort scale, pathway-level overinterpretation risks
sources.notes: Use as metadata/source-pattern support for cancer pathway interpretation; no excerpts until license is verified.

sources.title: Best practices for single-cell analysis across modalities
sources.journal_or_body: Nature Reviews Genetics / Single-cell Best Practices Consortium
sources.license: Springer Nature rights statement; metadata only unless article-level reuse is separately verified
sources.evidence_scope: single-cell multimodal analysis, scATAC-seq, CITE-seq, adaptive immune receptor repertoire, spatial information, integration, modality-specific preprocessing, benchmark-dependent tool recommendations
sources.notes: Use to route multimodal single-cell checks; do not require any single workflow, software package, or integration method.

sources.title: Adaptive Immune Receptor Repertoire Community recommendations for sharing immune-repertoire sequencing data
sources.license: author manuscript in PMC; metadata only unless reuse terms are separately verified
sources.evidence_scope: AIRR-seq sharing, MiAIRR minimal metadata, sample and data processing metadata, repository submission, immune repertoire provenance
sources.notes: Use for immune-repertoire metadata and sharing checks; do not apply to ordinary RNA-seq or all immunology text.

sources.title: AIRR Community Standardized Representations for Annotated Immune Repertoires
sources.evidence_scope: annotated immune receptor repertoire representation, AIRR schema, TSV representation, API/repository interoperability, alignment and metadata schema roadmap, provenance and tool/database interoperability
sources.notes: Use as source-pattern support for immune-repertoire sequence annotation and metadata provenance; not a scoring rubric.

sources.title: AIRR Standards documentation
sources.license: official standards documentation; metadata only unless documentation license is separately verified
sources.evidence_scope: MiAIRR standard, AIRR data model, immune receptor repertoire metadata, data representations, repository APIs, software guidelines and data commons interoperability
sources.notes: Use as current AIRR standards routing support; verify versioned documentation during specialization.

sources.title: Opportunities and challenges in long-read sequencing data analysis
sources.license: Genome Biology open access article; verify article-level license before storing excerpts
sources.evidence_scope: long-read sequencing analysis, platform-specific error models, basecalling, QC, error correction, alignment/assembly, structural variant calling, base modification detection, transcriptomics, benchmark and scalability limitations
sources.notes: Use to route long-read reporting checks; do not treat long reads as automatically superior for every genomics question.

sources.title: Data-independent acquisition-based SWATH-MS for quantitative proteomics: a tutorial
sources.license: open access article; verify article-level license before storing excerpts
sources.evidence_scope: DIA/SWATH-MS experiment planning, acquisition windows, spectral libraries or library-free strategies, peptide-centric scoring, quantitative consistency, instrument and software provenance
sources.notes: Use as DIA proteomics source-pattern support; do not require SWATH-MS for all proteomics studies.

sources.title: Principles of phosphoproteomics and applications in cancer research
sources.evidence_scope: phosphoproteomics in cancer research, phosphorylation-site interpretation, kinase/signaling inference, sample preparation, enrichment, mass spectrometry acquisition, computational interpretation, translational and mechanistic limitations
sources.notes: Use for phosphoproteomics claim calibration; phosphorylation-site abundance or kinase enrichment alone does not prove causal signaling.

sources.title: MITI minimum information guidelines for highly multiplexed tissue images
sources.license: Nature Methods article; metadata only unless reuse terms are separately verified
sources.evidence_scope: minimum information for highly multiplexed tissue imaging, sample/tissue metadata, imaging acquisition, channel/marker metadata, segmentation, image-derived data, repository and provenance requirements
sources.notes: Use only for highly multiplexed tissue imaging or related imaging-based spatial assays; do not apply to ordinary figures or non-imaging spatial data.

sources.title: Minimum Information about Tissue Imaging (MITI) reporting guidelines
sources.evidence_scope: machine-readable minimum metadata guidance for highly multiplexed tissue images, centralized and distributed data storage, imaging metadata standards
sources.notes: Use as routing and metadata standard support for multiplexed tissue imaging; verify versioned MITI schema during specialization.
